articles.headline: Ulta Beauty Appoints Kelly Garcia As Chief Technology Officer
articles.summary: Ulta Beauty has appointed Kelly Garcia as Chief Technology Officer (CTO), strengthening its executive leadership team to support the company’s technology and digital growth strategy. Effective August 31, 2026, Garcia will join Ulta Beauty’s executive team after serving on the company’s Board of Directors since 2022, from which he will resign upon assuming the role. He most recently served as Executive Vice President and CTO at Domino’s Pizza, where he led the company’s global technology strategy for more than a decade. Garcia brings over 25 years of experience spanning e-commerce, customer loyalty, digital innovation, artificial intelligence and cybersecurity.

articles.headline: Estee Lauder Cos. Decides Not To Sell Dr.Jart, Too Faced & Smashbox
articles.author: Rachel Klemovitch
articles.summary: According to recent reports, Estee Lauder Companies (ELC) has decided against selling Dr.Jart, Too Faced, and Smashbox. Rather than selling the brands, ELC has explored different strategic options and chose to restructure the brands’ operating models. These restructurings will help increase growth and profitability. Too Faced will relocate its headquarters from Los Angeles to New York and join the company’s makeup cluster alongside MAC and Bobbi Brown with a significantly streamlined team. While no president has been named to oversee Too Faced, Lisa Sequino is currently the president of the makeup cluster that Too Faced joins.

articles.headline: L’Oréal, MAC, And Clinique Fans Warned Of New Law BANNING Makeup Products Over Hidden Ingredient
articles.author: Grace Harrington
articles.summary: TOP makeup brands are being forced to abide by a new statewide law banning so-called forever chemicals from beauty products. The law will affect L’Oréal, MAC, Clinique and all other makeup manufacturers. A new Illinois law is banning the intentional addition of polyfluoroalkyl substances, or PFAS, in cosmetics, dental floss, juvenile products, menstrual products, and intimate apparel. PFAS are synthetic chemicals designed to make products resistant to heat, water, oil, or stains.

articles.headline: After The Estée Lauder-Puig Collapse, Which Beauty Mergers Would Actually Work?
articles.author: Rachel Brown
articles.summary: The proposed marriage between Estée Lauder and Puig collapsed in May before it was ever consummated. Since the engagement was called off, Lauder’s shares have climbed roughly 4%. Puig’s shares, however, are down more than 4%. The collapse underscored a blunt reality: the merger that made sense on paper (Lauder would gain enviable fragrance assets while Puig would achieve greater scale) quickly unraveled when confronted with the complexities of integration. Without rehashing all the sordid details of the blame game, from Charlotte Tilbury’s contract negotiations to disagreements over price, the deal ultimately appeared too messy for both the companies and investors, who wanted portfolio clarity and discipline but instead saw a clash of family-controlled dynasties and the prospect of prolonged post-acquisition pain.

articles.headline: L’Oréal Becomes The Exclusive Beauty Partner Of The Plenitude Arena
articles.summary: The L’Oréal Group and the Plenitude Arena announce the signing of an exclusive, renewable two-year partnership. This partnership places the world leader in beauty and its 40 international brands at the heart of the world’s greatest cultural and sporting events, hosted by Europe’s largest indoor venue (up to 45,000 seats), located in Nanterre, on the outskirts of Paris. Recently renamed Plenitude Arena as of July 1, 2026, the venue has just begun a new chapter in its history, driven by renewed ambitions and the arrival of leading partners to support its world-renowned events.

articles.headline: Chanel Dares To Rework Its Iconic Watch: Will The New J12 Make Us Forget The Original?
articles.author: Mila Lazri
articles.summary: The Chanel J12 Calibre 12.1 38 mm opens a new chapter for a watch that has become one of the Parisian house’s most recognizable models. More than twenty-five years after its launch, Chanel has chosen to update its icon without disrupting the silhouette that built its success. This new interpretation joins a collection created in 2000 under the artistic direction of Jacques Helleu. Inspired by racing cars and J-Class sailing yachts, the J12 helped establish ceramic in the world of high-end sports watches. Following the glossy black of the original version and the white model introduced in 2003, Chanel is now exploring a more understated expression of its flagship watch.Produits de luxe

articles.headline: Gucci Claimed A Monte Carlo Beach Club Through October: 4 More Luxury Pop-Ups Still Open
articles.author: Edward Mills
articles.summary: This is mid-July, which means you are squarely inside the window. Six luxury fashion houses have taken over some of the most storied beach clubs and coastal hotels in Europe — from Monaco to Portofino to Ibiza — and every single one of them is still open. The first to close, Burberry on the French Riviera and Chloé in Formentera, runs through September 30. Gucci's Monaco takeover and Jacquemus's Bodrum debut hold until the end of October. What used to be a niche marketing move — a branded sunbed here, a logo parasol there — has grown into a genuine travel phenomenon, with fashion houses now designing full seasonal programs around specific coastal addresses, right down to the cocktails, the bicycles, and the restaurant menus. Here is where they are, what each one looks like in practice, and the critical detail most coverage leaves out: which ones you can walk into without booking a room.

articles.headline: Ulta Beauty Welcomes Monpure London
articles.author: Rachel Klemovitch
articles.summary: Monpure London, the trichologist-backed British hair and scalp health brand, adds Ulta Beauty to a growing U.S. retail roster that already includes Nordstrom, Bloomingdale’s and Mybeautyspace.com. Scalp health has shifted from niche interest to mainstream priority in the U.S., with bond repair following close behind. Monpure’s position, built on clinical research and trichologist insight rather than trend, places it among a small number of brands with the credibility to lead that shift at retail.

articles.headline: Ingredient-Led Brands Are Dominating AI Beauty Citations
articles.author: Emily Jensen
articles.summary: The Ordinary shook up the beauty aisle when it launched in 2016 with affordable skin-care formulations built around a single active ingredient, like hyaluronic acid or caffeine. A decade later, that approach appears to be helping the Estée Lauder-owned skin-care brand in a new era of beauty consumption. According to data from 5W AI Communications, The Ordinary tops the list of beauty brands surfaced in AI citations. The brand’s products appeared in 7% of responses to beauty-related consumer prompts submitted to ChatGPT, Claude, Perplexity and Google AI Overviews. Skin-care brands largely dominated the top results, with CeraVe and La Roche-Posay also in the top five brands by AI citations.

articles.headline: Glamsquad Is Redefining The Beauty Experience
articles.summary: David Goldweitz, cofounder and chief executive officer of Glamsquad, and Giovanni Vaccaro, cofounder of Glamsquad, discuss with Fairchild Studio how the beauty services platform is shaping the future of beauty experiences with tech-driven personalization and brand partnerships.

articles.headline: Wellness Briefing: Is Hormone Tracking The Future Of Women’s Wearables? Plus News
articles.author: Lexy Lebsack
articles.summary: For the Wellness Briefing, Glossy interviewed Jenny Duan, the 22-year-old founder who has raised $11.6 million to launch a continuous hormone-tracking wearable named Clair. It’s part of the burgeoning hormone-tracking marketplace that could soon upend how women interact with wearables. Additionally, NYC Mayor Zohran Mamdani announces a new “click to cancel” law that will soon impact gyms and subscription-based CPG. Plus, a new stat illustrates spa worker burnout, and CorePower Yoga and Gold’s Gym experience exec shakeups.

articles.headline: Influencer Marketing Insights: Traackr And Unilever Discuss Creator Collaborations
articles.summary: At the WWD Beauty CEO Summit, Pierre-Loïc Assayag, cofounder and chief executive officer of Traackr, and Casey DePalma McCartney, chief strategy and brand communications officer at Unilever U.S., sat down with Amanda Smith, CEO of Fairchild Media Group, to share how influencer marketing is evolving. This special episode of WWD Voices features their live discussion from the mainstage. During the panel, speakers noted the importance of establishing relationships with creators and social listening, which together enable brands to react to trending online conversations quickly and authentically.

articles.headline: From Chanel To Dior, The Greek Goddess Effect Endures
articles.author: Tonya Blazio-Licorish
articles.summary: Zendaya’s recent red carpet appearance for summer’s presumed next blockbuster film “The Odyssey” reminded us how Greek dress styles have consistently inspired designer collections through the years. From Chanel to Dior and everywhere in between, Grecian-style draping, flowing pleats and bare-shoulder looks in floor-length to mini silhouettes may echo the past, but continue to resonate as one of fashion’s most enduring sources of inspiration. Across fashion’s timelines, dress codes from ancient Greece — from the chiton to the peplos — have created statement-making moments on and off the runway. While early successes can easily be connected to French couturier Madame Grès, whose meticulously draped and pleated column evening dresses remain iconic, many designers have since added to the conversation.

articles.headline: Ulta Beauty Hit With Patent Suit Over Store Locator Features
articles.author: Riya Sharma
articles.summary: Ulta Salon, Cosmetics & Fragrance, which does business as Ulta Beauty, was accused of patent infringement over location-based wireless service methods. The beauty retailer used a store locator feature on its app and website that allegedly infringed on two patents — US Nos. 7,532,899 and 8,774,834 — owned by non-practicing entity Local Interest LLC that cover methods for locating sets of desired locations. The complaint was filed Wednesday in the US District Court for the Eastern District of Texas.

articles.headline: The Estée Lauder Companies Appoints Madeleine Boyd As Senior Vice President, Global Brand Communications
articles.summary: The Estée Lauder Companies Inc. (NYSE: EL) today announced the appointment of Madeleine Boyd as Senior Vice President, Global Brand Communications, effective July 20, 2026. As part of the company’s continued efforts to strengthen how its brands better connect with consumers, Ms. Boyd will establish and lead a newly integrated Global Brand Communications team. In this role, she will ensure the company’s diverse portfolio is anchored by a cohesive enterprise communications strategy, while accelerating bold, consumer-first storytelling that drives earned media, cultural relevance, and brand desirability. She will also strengthen creator engagement, helping the company’s brands gain attention where culture is being shaped.

articles.headline: Chanel Says No More Shredding As Unsold Goods Shift To L’Atelier Des Matières
articles.author: Tianwei Zhang
articles.summary: After a court disclosure revealed thousands of products were shredded in Hong Kong every six months back in 2017, Chanel points to L’Atelier des Matières and new circular unit Nevold as proof that unsold goods are now recycled, not destroyed. Chanel is pushing back against figures recently disclosed in a Hong Kong courtroom, stressing they don’t reflect how the brand now handles unsold goods as Europe tightens the rules on fashion waste.

articles.headline: Chanel CFO Philippe Blondiaux To Retire At End Of 2026
articles.summary: The longtime finance executive will step down after 15 years with the luxury house, with a successor to be announced at a later date. Chanel global chief financial officer Philippe Blondiaux will retire at the end of 2026, concluding a 15-year tenure at the privately held luxury house. Blondiaux joined Chanel in 2011 following an international career spanning finance and business leadership roles at Nestlé and PwC. Over the following decade and a half, he became one of the company’s most visible senior executives, regularly representing Chanel in discussions of its financial performance, investment strategy and long-term approach to growth.

articles.headline: Destroy Or Resell: The Impossible Equation For Luxury Giants Facing Their Own Stocks
articles.author: Julie Boone
articles.summary: Long kept as one of the best-guarded secrets in the luxury industry, the destruction of unsold goods is now caught between ethical questioning and legislative constraints. Behind the scenes of haute couture, prestige has long been measured by what did not sell. To preserve this sense of exclusivity, destroying exceptional pieces rather than discounting them was a tacit but absolute rule. Today, faced with societal pressure and unprecedented regulations, the industry must learn to manage its end-of-cycle products without damaging its image. It is a paradox that has long plagued the fashion industry: artisans spend dozens of hours crafting exceptional bags and garments, only for these very objects to sometimes end up shredded or incinerated in the secrecy of high-security industrial zones.

articles.headline: Luxury Retailer’s Employee Criminal Trial Exposes Industry Practice
articles.author: Fernanda Tronco
articles.summary: One of the world’s most recognizable luxury fashion brands is at the center of a yearslong criminal trial after two former warehouse employees were accused of stealing hundreds of high-end products that had been designated for destruction. Beyond the alleged theft, testimony in the case has pulled back the curtain on a little-known practice in the luxury industry: destroying unsold merchandise to preserve exclusivity rather than allowing it to be sold at discounted prices.

articles.headline: Treatment Stacking To TPE: The Next Aesthetic Treatment Trends To Know
articles.author: Amy Francombe
articles.summary: The modern aesthetics industry, long plagued by fillers, lasers, Botox, and the nips and tucks of aging skin, is sitting on the precipice of its next era. As the age-old act of going under the knife retreats into the background, the next cohort of treatments is waiting in the wings. But this time, the focus goes even deeper than an injectable, with more consumers becoming hyperfixated on skin quality, collagen stimulation, inflammation, hormonal changes, metabolic health, or how to preserve their appearance over decades, not just years.

articles.headline: Regulations Have Been Developed For The Beauty Industry
articles.author: Tatiana Sichirliiscaia
articles.summary: The State Tax Service has developed guidelines for providing beauty services. The document is intended to help individuals formalize their business activities. Today, they can operate under the specialty “Hairdressing and Beauty Salon Services,” as defined in the Classification of Economic Activities, and also carry out the activity “Hairdressing and Cosmetic Services” under a business license. The document is available on the tax authority’s official website. It includes information on the types of economic activities in this field. It is emphasized, however, that this does not include the provision of medical services.

articles.headline: Paris Hilton Returns To NYX Professional Makeup As The Gloss Boss
articles.author: Lollie Hancock
articles.summary: NYX Professional Makeup has named socialite-turned-entrepreneur Paris Hilton as its new Gloss Boss. The appointment comes as part of the next chapter of the L’Oréal-owned make-up brand’s annual campaign, ‘If You NYX, You Know’ (IYNYK). Hilton, who was part of the first installment of IYNYK, which debuted last summer, is welcomed back amongst a roster of celebrity partners, including pop star and ‘Love Lies’ singer Normani, Love Island UK’s Toni Laites, Brazilian model and content creator Gabriela Moura and Middle Eastern beauty creator Yusur Al-khalidi.

articles.headline: Gucci Racing Appoints VP
articles.author: Luisa Zargani
articles.summary: Tancredi Vitale will join Gucci from the Venezia Football Club, where he served as managing director, on Sept. 1. Gucci Racing has named Tancredi Vitale its vice president, effective Sept. 1, joining from the Venezia Football Club, where he served as managing director.

articles.headline: Chanel Just Found Its New Crush, And Her Name Is Gracie Abrams
articles.author: Amaya Garcia
articles.summary: Mademoiselle Abrams has entered the chat. Chanel just announced Gracie Abrams as the new face of Coco Mademoiselle Crush Absolu, the house's newest fragrance, launching this August—and if you've been keeping tabs on her steady rise through the Chanel universe, this one tracks perfectly.The 26-year-old singer has been a Chanel ambassador since 2025, fronting the Spring-Summer Pre-Collection campaign before becoming the face of Coco Crush jewelry. Landing Crush Absolu feels like the natural next chapter, a continuation of a relationship that's clearly working for both sides. "It's unreal to be the new face of Coco Mademoiselle, I feel an immense sense of pride," Abrams said in a press release. "I love that Coco is someone who leaves a mark everywhere she goes. Sometimes, I wish I could be more like her."

articles.headline: Ulta Beauty Names Domino’s Veteran As CTO
articles.author: Paige Gross
articles.summary: Ulta Beauty has named a new CTO in Kelly Garcia, a current board member who will begin his new role Aug. 31, the company announced Tuesday. As CTO, Garcia will continue to advance the beauty retailer’s technology capabilities and its tech-driven Ulta Beauty Unleashed strategy, which aims to drive market share growth. Garcia will leave his seat on Ulta’s board to take the position, as well as his 14-year tenure at Domino’s, where he served most recently as the company’s EVP and CTO. “Kelly has been an impactful member of our Board of Directors, providing valuable business insights and technology expertise, including perspectives on emerging technologies such as AI,” Kecia Steelman, president and CEO, said in a statement.

articles.headline: Publicis Groupe Wins Gucci And Balenciaga Global Media Account
articles.author: Shauna Lewis
articles.summary: Gucci owner Kering has appointed Publicis Groupe to its global media account, following a competitive review led by ID Comms. The incumbent on the account was Dentsu's iProspect, which was appointed in 2021, following a competitive review in which Publicis agency Zenith unsuccessfully defended the account. In 2025, the company's media spend totalled $319.9m (£240.2m), according to COMvergence, and in 2026 it is predicted to spend less, at $293.3m (£220.2m). As well as Gucci, Kering’s brands include Balenciaga, Bottega Veneta, Stella McQueen, Saint Laurent and Brioni.

articles.headline: L’Oréal Paris’ New Revitalift Serum Reverses Five Years Of Dark Spots
articles.author: Lianna Albrizo
articles.summary: As consumers brave the worst of summer heat waves brought on by blankets of harsh sunlight, it also means the development of dreaded dark spots. Happi International Top 30 Company L’Oréal Paris says it has the antidote: Revitalift Melasyl Dark Spot Creamy-Serum. The innovation, two decades in the making, is formulated to visibly correct dark spots, improve skin tone uniformity and smooth wrinkles. Melasyl, the latest addition to L’Oréal Groupe’s Revitalift franchise, utilizes a patented cosmetic ingredient.

articles.headline: Coty (COTY) Is Up 10.7% After Handing Back Gucci Beauty License To Kering For US$400 Million
articles.summary: In early July 2026, Coty Inc. announced it would transition the Gucci Beauty license back to Kering for about US$400 million, continue operating the brand through at least June 30, 2027, and use the proceeds for debt reduction, core prestige investments, and organizational streamlining. The deal not only reshapes Coty’s prestige portfolio by exiting a key licensed brand but also resolves all related litigation with Kering and accelerates Coty’s shift toward a more focused, centrally managed operating model.

articles.headline: Brands Urge TikTok Shop Creators To Ditch AI Fakes, Report Says
articles.author: Erin Keller
articles.summary: AI-generated shopping videos are spreading across TikTok Shop, raising concerns among brands that the technology could misrepresent products and make it harder for shoppers to distinguish between authentic reviews and computer-generated promotions. AI video tools now allow creators and sellers to quickly produce product demonstrations, virtual influencers and promotional clips without filming real people or using physical products. While TikTok has rolled out AI features to help sellers create shoppable content, some brands are setting limits on how their products can appear in these videos.

articles.headline: How Beauty Retailers Are Winning the Attention Economy
articles.author: Larry Stansbury
articles.summary: For decades, beauty brands largely relied on editors, publishers, and magazines to introduce new products, educate consumers, and shape conversations before the retailer served as the point of purchase. Today, that model is evolving. As beauty discovery becomes increasingly fragmented across TikTok, Amazon, AI-powered search tools, creators, and brick and mortar, beauty retailers are competing for consumers’ attention long before they make a purchase. The goal is not simply to sell the products but to foster a community and connection that keep consumers engaged before and well after a purchase occurs. Retailers that successfully maintain that relationship are more likely to

articles.headline: K-Beauty Value Sales Jump 53% Year On Year Finds New NIQ Report
articles.author: Isabelle Crossley
articles.summary: K-Beauty value sales increased by 53% year on year and 131% over two years as the product category continues to grow in global popularity, according to a new report by consumer intelligence business NIQ Global Intelligence plc titled ‘K-Beauty Goes Global.’ NIQ attributes Korean beauty brands’ category growth to digitally driven consumer demand and strong regional innovation, the business announced in a release on Wednesday. Social commerce also plays a key role in the acceleration of K-Beauty in global markets. Citing data published by TikTok Shop, NIQ notes that searches for K-Beauty on the platform increased by 125% in the UK in 2025. TikTok Shop also reported a 430% year on year increase in value sales for K-Beauty brands in the UK, US, Spain, and Germany.

articles.headline: Bustle’s 2026 Big Life Beauty Awards
articles.summary: Beauty doesn’t exist in a vacuum — it exists in the middle of real life. It’s the sunscreen stick you aggressively reapply while sprinting through the airport, the concealer that makes you look awake during your third Zoom call of the day, the hairspray that somehow survives humidity and a chaotic commute, and the body oil that convinces you your everything shower counts as self-care (because honestly, it does). For Bustle’s 2026 Big Life Beauty Awards, we wanted to celebrate the products that don’t just sit prettily on your vanity, but actually show up for you. The formulas that survive sweaty dance floors, long workdays, beach trips, bad breakouts, red-eye flights, emotional support baths, and every overbooked calendar moment in between.

articles.headline: Neutrogena® Announces ‘Break The Rules,’ A New Brand Mission Empowering Consumers To Break Beauty’s Rules With Science
articles.summary: New retinol campaign from Neutrogena® proves consumers don’t have to compromise, advancing the brand’s dermatologist-led innovation and culturally relevant storytelling. Break the Rules debuts July 15, 2026, and unites the brand’s dermatologist-backed science, consumer-first approach, and breakthrough product development under one clear brand mission: Neutrogena® breaks the rules with science, so consumers can break the rules of beauty. Social listening (Pulsar) found nearly 17,000 U.S. consumer posts about retinol on TikTok and Instagram between January–May 2026, consistently surfacing a core tension: consumers want retinol’s results without the irritation

articles.headline: Is Beauty’s Refill Movement Actually Working?
articles.author: Nyima Jobe
articles.summary: Beauty brands have spent the past five years betting big on refillable products, but is the movement actually changing consumer buying habits From giants like L'Oréal Groupe, to smaller companies such as Wild and Neal's Yard Remedies, beauty businesses have been investing heavily in refill systems as they race to cut packaging waste and appeal to increasingly sustainability-conscious consumers. L'Oréal Groupe alone has increased its refillable beauty offering 3.7-fold between 2019 and 2025, while the latest iteration of its #JoinTheRefillMovement campaign now spans 18 brands across all four of its divisions – Luxe, Consumer, Professional and Dermatological. But while refill options are multiplying across prestige and mass beauty alike, one question remains: are shoppers actually changing the way they buy beauty?

articles.headline: ‘Chasing Dual Excellence’: Why L’Oréal Believes Growth And Responsibility Go Hand-In-Hand
articles.author: Matilda Cox
articles.summary: L’Oréal Groupe's chief corporate responsibility officer, Ezgi Barcenas, sits down with edie to discuss why and how the company aims to make an impact beyond commercial performance, from supporting start-ups to philanthropic funding.

articles.headline: Madeleine Boyd Joins The Estée Lauder Group To Boost Brand Desirability
articles.summary: Amid the ongoing restructuring of its brand portfolio, The Estée Lauder Companies has appointed Madeleine Boyd as Senior Vice President, Global Brand Communications. She will take up the position on July 20, 2026. In her new role, Ms. Boyd will establish and lead a newly integrated Global Brand Communications team. She will oversee the development of a unified communications strategy across The Estée Lauder Companies’ diverse brand portfolio, while advancing bold, consumer-centric storytelling designed to enhance earned media, cultural relevance, and brand desirability. Ms. Boyd brings extensive experience spanning brand strategy, communications, consumer engagement, and cultural insights across the beauty, luxury, and lifestyle sectors.

articles.headline: Cosmetics Business’ 5 Dream Beauty Partnerships And Collaborations
articles.author: Lollie Hancock
articles.summary: With beauty brands announcing A-list partnerships and cool collaborations on the daily, the Cosmetics Business team have dreamt up their fantasy crossovers, from unlikely video game and make-up collaborations, to seeing actors Paul Rudd and Danny Devito fronting campaigns. Paris Hilton and NYX Professional Makeup, Gracie Abrams for Chanel, L’Oréal Paris x Elle and… Danny DeVito fronting a hair campaign? With celebrity endorsements, ambassadorships and collaborations announced by beauty brands almost on the daily, it is clear that the industry truly sees value in bringing on a famous face

articles.headline: Chanel Opens Standalone Fragrance & Beauty Boutique At YYZ
articles.author: Naomi Chadderton
articles.summary: Chanel has strengthened its travel retail presence in North America with the opening of a standalone Fragrance & Beauty boutique at Toronto Pearson International Airport’s Terminal 1. The new boutique, opened with Avolta, has been designed to deliver an immersive and personalised beauty experience for international travellers, combining the House’s heritage with a contemporary retail concept inspired by summer.

articles.headline: Amazon Is Reinvigorating Its Beauty Shopping Experience
articles.summary: Fairchild Studio: What are you excited about for your new role at Amazon in health and beauty? What’s the trajectory for 2026? Wendy Franks: Health and beauty is such a dynamic category and one where we’re seeing incredible innovation and momentum at Amazon. We’ve gone from being a destination for replenishing everyday products to a more elevated experience, where customers can discover prestige brands, indie products and complete their entire health and beauty regimen in one stop. Beauty is one of the fastest-growing categories at Amazon — and with that comes an outsized opportunity to innovate. We’re leaning into artificial intelligence and personalization to make discovery more seamless and relevant, while continuing to expand our wide assortment of brands, especially in the fragrance category, where we have some exciting developments coming this year.

articles.headline: Korean Brands Touch Down In LA For Monthlong Retail Showcase
articles.author: Lee Kyung-min
articles.summary: A Korean retail platform has brought a slice of Seoul to one of Los Angeles’ busiest shopping destinations, creating a monthlong stage for small and midsize Korean brands seeking a foothold in the United States. The Korea Trade-Investment Promotion Agency (KOTRA) said Monday it is operating a K-beauty and lifestyle pop-up store with Lotte Home Shopping at The Grove in Los Angeles from July 17 to Aug. 15. The event features 39 Korean small and midsize companies aiming to expand into the U.S. market through a mix of consumer experiences and business matchmaking.

articles.headline: How Much Unsold Merchandise Do Luxury Brands Destroy Every Year?
articles.summary: As the EU’s July 19 ban on destroying unsold clothing takes effect, a Chanel theft trial in Hong Kong reveals the scale of luxury’s disposal habit — and the recycling infrastructure brands are racing to build. During the Tuesday trial of two former warehouse staffers accused of trying to steal 724 items earmarked for destruction, prosecutors in Hong Kong told the court that Chanel’s local operation destroys between 10,000 and 20,000 products every six months. The figure came up as evidence in a property case, but it also put a specific number on something luxury houses have been doing for decades, which is to systematically destroy the goods they have decided not to sell.

articles.headline: Korean Beauty Products Are Becoming Mainstream In The U.S. Why There May Be Even More Growth Ahead
articles.author: Sean Conlon
articles.summary: When Olive Young opened its first U.S. outpost in late May, shoppers were already camping out, and the line spanned multiple blocks. On opening weekend, the leading South Korean beauty retailer’s new store in Pasadena, California, had 6,000 customers move through its doors, and it currently sees an average of more than 1,600 visitors per day, the company said. It has since opened another location, in Century City, California, and said it plans to open more stores in the U.S. That popularity is a microcosm of a bigger trend playing out in the U.S.: Consumers can’t get enough of beauty products from a country thousands of miles away — positioning some companies to get ahead.

articles.headline: Nobody Dupes A Bad Brand. That’s Why Chanel Should Love Ballet Flat Knockoffs
articles.author: Robin Landa
articles.summary: Among fashion-fluent women, the Chanel ballet flat has become stratified. The iconic beige-and-black cap toe, once the grail, is now so common among the style-literate that insiders hunt baby pinks, candy reds, sky blues, denim, and embossed leather. The shoe that once marked the club now distinguishes rank within it. That’s what peak brand power looks like. There’s another tribute to that power. Department stores are full of ballet flats that look remarkably similar for under $100. So what, exactly, is the other $1,000 buying? The answer explains why successful brands become cultural forces.

articles.headline: No Gucci, No Problem? Coty Learns To Live Without Flagship Brand
articles.author: Alexander Marrow and Lisa Jucca
articles.summary: Coty's (COTY.N), opens new tab $400 million deal to exit its flagship Gucci Beauty licence a year early will cost it a big chunk of near-term ‌earnings, but could prove a turning point for the struggling beauty brand under interim CEO Markus Strobel. The decision to hand back its most prestigious licence means fiscal year 2028 will be rough for the Procter & Gamble veteran, who took over in January from former boss Sue Nabi at a group whose shares have already slid 80% since early 2024.

articles.headline: Ulta Beauty Hires Kelly Garcia As Chief Technology Officer
articles.summary: Ulta Beauty Inc. has appointed Kelly Garcia, a company board member, as its chief technology officer, effective August 31. On that date, Garcia will give up the board seat he has held since 2022. “Kelly has been an impactful member of our board of directors, providing valuable business insights and technology expertise, including perspectives on emerging technologies such as AI, and we are excited to enhance our executive team with his leadership as our new CTO,” said Kecia Steelman, president and chief executive officer at Ulta.

articles.headline: Cosmetics Giant Estée Lauder Victim Of Mass Oracle Breach
articles.author: Alex Scroxton
articles.summary: Cosmetics multinational Estée Lauder is contacting both current and former employees after discovering internal data was compromised in a breach orchestrated through Oracle’s E-Business Suite (EBS) service in the summer of 2025. The New York-based Estée Lauder group told victims that on or around 9 August 2025, an unauthorised third-party gained access to its EBS system and was able to obtain data including names, postal and email addresses, dates of birth, US Social Security numbers, passport data, bank details, health information, and internal human resources (HR) data such as payroll and performance reviews.

articles.headline: Madeleine Boyd Named SVP Global Brand Communications At Estée Lauder
articles.author: Christine Schott Ledes
articles.summary: The Estée Lauder Companies Inc. has announced the appointment of Madeleine Boyd as Senior Vice President, Global Brand Communications, effective July 20, 2026. As part of the company’s continued efforts to strengthen how its brands better connect with consumers, Boyd will establish and lead a newly integrated Global Brand Communications team. In this role, she will ensure the company’s diverse portfolio is anchored by a cohesive enterprise communications strategy, while accelerating bold, consumer-first storytelling that drives earned media, cultural relevance, and brand desirability. She will also strengthen creator engagement, helping the company’s brands gain attention where culture is being shaped.

articles.headline: Ulta Beauty vs. Sally Beauty: A Comparison Of Recent Revenue Trends
articles.author: John Ballard
articles.summary: Ulta Beauty consistently generates higher revenue totals and demonstrates a generally upward longer-term trajectory compared to Sally Beauty. Over the last eight quarters, Ulta Beauty experienced notable quarter-over-quarter fluctuations, while Sally Beauty maintained a highly stable revenue baseline. Investors should watch whether the revenue gap between the two companies continues to widen or begins to stabilize in upcoming quarters.

articles.headline: New Report Analyzes AI Visibility In ChatGPT Responses
articles.author: Rachel Klemovitch
articles.summary: Avenue Z, a digital marketing and PR agency specializing in AI visibility and Answer Engine Optimization (AEO), released its 2026 AI Visibility Index (AIVx) Beauty Report. The report analyzes AI visibility, rank, and citation trends across 60 brands and 565 AI citations derived from ChatGPT responses. Whitney Hart, Chief Strategy Officer and Director of the AI Lab at Avenue Z, said: “Beauty brands need to understand that AI visibility is not a content volume game, rather it is a credibility game. In this category, AI visibility is being built through authority that exists beyond a brand’s own channels. The leaders are gaining ground because trusted third parties are consistently shaping how AI understands and recommends them. That is a very different playbook from traditional brand publishing, and it is quickly becoming a real competitive advantage.”

articles.headline: How Brands Can Create Meaning In The Age Of AI
articles.author: Amy O’Brien
articles.summary: In 1935, German philosopher Walter Benjamin warned that mechanical reproduction would erode the “aura” of art. By allowing paintings and photographs to be copied endlessly, he argued, technology detached cultural objects from the unique contexts (time and place) that gave them meaning. Half a century later, Andy Warhol’s factory flipped this theory on its head. By reproducing Campbell’s soup cans and Marilyn Monroe portraits at industrial scale, Warhol collapsed the distinction between commerce and culture, suggesting that mass production and repetition could become a form of value in itself.

articles.headline: Creator And Influencer Trends Brand Marketers Need To Know About Right Now
articles.author: Gillian Follett
articles.summary: Each week, we highlight the latest developments at the intersection between brands and the creator economy, including new partnerships between brands and creators, notable new features across social media platforms and the growth of creator-led businesses.

articles.headline: The New Beauty Goal Isn’t Looking Younger. It’s Skin Longevity.
articles.summary: For decades, the beauty industry sold us the same promise: look younger. Erase the lines.

articles.headline: Cosmoprof North America Signals Beauty’s Next Growth Drivers, From Smarter SPF To Fragrance And K-Beauty
articles.author: Ryma Chikhoune
articles.summary: Cosmoprof North America returned to Las Vegas July 13 to 15 as retailers, brands, manufacturers and suppliers gathered to gauge where beauty innovation and consumer demand are headed next. Across the show floor, several themes surfaced, from next-generation sunscreen technology and clinically backed, problem-solving products and tools to fragrance’s expansion beyond perfume and K-beauty’s continued evolution. Together, it reflected a beauty market defined by performance, specialization and products that deliver measurable results.

articles.headline: Which Beauty Ingredients Are Trending Most In South Korea?
articles.author: Noor Lobad
articles.summary: Is azulene the next PDRN? Data from Spate detailing the top 10 beauty ingredients by monthly search growth via South Korea’s Naver shows that azulene ranks in the top three, trailing behind already-mainstream counterparts PDRN and minoxidil, respectively. Azulene is an anti-inflammatory compound derived from plants like chamomile and yarrow. Known to calm sensitive skin, the ingredient garnered roughly 15,300 monthly searches on Naver from June 2025 through May 2026, an increase of 29 percent versus the year prior.

articles.headline: K-Beauty’s Global Boom: Sales Up 131% In Two Years, Says NIQ
articles.summary: New data from the consumer intelligence firm shows K-Beauty global value sales rose 53% year-over-year and 131% over two years. In its latest report, K-Beauty Goes Global, NIQ shows how Korean beauty is reshaping consumer expectations, accelerating innovation cycles and redefining competitive dynamics across the global beauty market. According to the market research firm, K-Beauty has become a rapidly growing global beauty segment, with value sales up 53% year-over-year and 131% over the past two years.

articles.headline: Sephora Introduces Global ‘Quiet Hours’ Shopping Experience, A First For Beauty Retailers
articles.author: Iman Palm
articles.summary: Sephora has introduced “Quiet Hours,” a new initiative designed to offer a calmer in-store shopping experience for customers who prefer reduced sensory stimulation. The beauty retailer says it is the first in its industry to roll out “Quiet Hours” globally. During designated times, stores will lower music volume and adjust digital screens to create a quieter environment with fewer distractions. Sephora said the program was shaped with input from neurodiversity advocates, including Open Inclusion and Purposeful Futures, as well as internal employee resource groups.
